evals.prompt: Create the agent workflow for this project. I want the full multi-agent pipeline.
evals.expected_output: The skill activates BOOTSTRAP mode: creates 7 agent files, 1 instruction file, 3 task files, and verifies the result with a directory listing.
assertions: Detects BOOTSTRAP mode from the user's request
Creates all 7 agent files in .github/agents/
Creates tech-lead-workflow.instructions.md in .github/instructions/
Creates lessons.md, todo.md, and summaries.md in .github/tasks/
Checks for existing files before overwriting
Verifies creation with a directory listing
Reports the summary of created files

evals.prompt: I need to add OAuth authentication to my Next.js app. It must support Google and GitHub as providers, protect admin routes, and store the session in a secure cookie.
evals.expected_output: The workflow activates REFERENCE mode with multi-agent planning: Planner decomposes into subtasks (OAuth config, providers, middleware, session), Implementer codes with GPT-5.4, Reviewer verifies security, Tester runs tests.
assertions: Uses the structured handoff protocol with TASK, COMPLEXITY, SKILLS, ACCEPTANCE
Delegates to Planner before implementing
Includes COMPLEXITY: high in the handoff
The Reviewer verifies auth and cookie security
The flow follows the order: Plan → Implement → Test → Review
Lessons are recorded in ### [CATEGORY] Title format

evals.prompt: The contact form doesn't send emails. Users report it stays loading forever after submit.
evals.expected_output: The workflow identifies this as a bug-fix, delegates investigation to the Planner for diagnosis, then Implementer fixes, Tester verifies the fix.
assertions: Correctly classifies as bug-fix
Uses the handoff protocol with relevant LESSONS_FILTER
The Implementer investigates the root cause before patching
The Tester verifies the form works post-fix
New lessons are recorded in lessons.md with ### [CATEGORY] Title format
Todo is tracked with ## Feature — date format

evals.prompt: I want to optimize my workflow costs. I'm spending too many premium requests on simple tasks.
evals.expected_output: The skill provides the model-per-agent cost table, explains LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH profiles, and recommends savings strategies based on the RLM paradigm.
assertions: References GitHub Copilot costs (multipliers 0.33x, 0.9x, 1x, 3x)
Explains that GPT-5.4 is the 1x baseline when wider context matters, with Haiku reserved for LOW tasks
Mentions the RLM paradigm as the optimization foundation
Presents LOW, MEDIUM, and HIGH profiles as options
Recommends NOT using free models because they don't load skills correctly
